title: Heal Group Membership on In-Page URL Drift
description: Grouped Google Docs tabs were being dropped from their group because membership is keyed by the full URL including the ?tab= query, which Google rewrites in-page via the History API — the recorded key drifted away from the live key, so exact-key eject/reconciliation paths concluded the tab was no longer a member. The onUpdated in-page branch now heals the drift: it locates the stale label urlKey by page identity (samePageKey) and rewrites it to the live getUrlKey value (or drops the stale slot to avoid a duplicate), so all downstream exact-key comparisons keep matching; real navigations still eject. Also added durable, runtime-toggleable grouping diagnostics: debugGroup now persists breadcrumbs to a capped 200-entry groupingLog ring buffer in chrome.storage.local (gated on a debugGrouping flag) that survives MV3 service-worker restarts, backed by a new pure appendGroupingLog helper, plus a breadcrumb at the previously-silent navigation-eject site.
